description: CIP-8 G2 replay fixture — byte-exact frozen ballot for round 29 (Polymarket MicroStrategy/BTC resolution). Recomputing ballotHash(question, frozenCriteria) reproduces the published round-29 ballot hash, proving the signed YES 3/3 verdict was bound to exactly these frozen criteria. Persisting this preimage is the CIP-8 §4 guarantee made checkable.
question: Independently judge how the Polymarket market 'MicroStrategy sells any Bitcoin by May 31, 2026?' should resolve, based ONLY on the market's ORIGINAL resolution criteria as frozen at creation (provided in the brief). Research/verify independently if you can. Two questions: (1) on the ORIGINAL rule, did a qualifying sale occur by 11:59 PM ET May 31, 2026? (2) Should the 'Additional context' that Polymarket posted on June 1 (after the SEC filing and after positions were taken) legitimately change the resolution, or is it a retroactive rule change that must be disregarded? Reason fully, cite the rule's own designated primary sources, then vote YES (resolves Yes on frozen criteria), NO (resolves No), or INDETERMINATE (original criteria genuinely cannot resolve this fairly). End with VERDICT: <token>.
frozenCriteria:
NEUTRAL EVIDENCE BRIEF (both sides; no conclusion drawn). Verify independently if you have research tools.

MARKET: Polymarket, "MicroStrategy sells any Bitcoin by May 31, 2026?" (~$85M volume; "in review" near 81% YES at dispute time).

ORIGINAL RESOLUTION RULE, as written at market creation (verbatim):
"This market will resolve to 'Yes' if MicroStrategy sells any of its Bitcoin by 11:59 PM ET on the date specified in the title."
Designated resolution sources: PRIMARY = MSTR's SEC filings and on-chain data; BACKUP = consensus of credible reporting.

FACTS (per reporting and SEC filing):
- Strategy (MicroStrategy) sold 32 BTC (~$2.5M) between May 26 and May 31, 2026.
- The Form 8-K disclosing this was FILED June 1, 2026 (after the May 31 deadline).
- That 8-K presents the holdings/activity "as of May 31, 2026, 4:00 p.m. Eastern Time" (i.e., the sale itself is dated within the window).
- On-chain transactions corresponding to the sale fall in the May 26-31 window.

THE DISPUTED ADDITION: On June 1, AFTER the 8-K filing, Polymarket posted "Additional context" to the market's rules page (verbatim):
"No information from MSTR, on-chain data, or consensus of credible reporting confirmed that MicroStrategy sold Bitcoin within the market's timeframe," and that "confirmation achieved outside of the market's timeframe does not qualify."
Effect reported: the market had spiked to ~81% YES on the filing, then collapsed to near zero after this addition.

THE TWO ARGUMENTS:
- YES side: the rule keys on when the SALE occurred; the sale occurred by the deadline; MSTR's own filing (a designated primary source) dates it within the window.
- NO side: no public information confirmed a sale before the June 1 filing; the addition says confirmation outside the timeframe does not qualify, so it should resolve NO.

MECHANISM: final resolution is by a binding UMA optimistic-oracle token-holder vote (48-96h); reporting notes concerns about concentrated voting power and conflicts of interest, and that Polymarket has logged 1,150+ disputed markets in 2026 already.

SOURCES: CoinDesk (2026-06-01), The Defiant, crypto.news, DeFi Rate, Bitcoinist, Yahoo Finance, Phemex (all June 2026).
